summary: Testing IDSEA Payment System integration (Iteration 9). Backend: 14/14 tests PASSED. Frontend: Admin Payment Settings page works correctly. CRITICAL BUG: Event registration payment flow (Step 5) is being skipped - button shows 'Confirm Registration' instead of 'Proceed to Payment' even when totalAmount > 0 (₹4000), causing users to bypass payment step.

ui_bugs.issue: Step 4 Review button shows 'Confirm Registration' instead of 'Proceed to Payment' when totalAmount > 0 (₹4000). The useMemo computed totalAmount is evaluated as 0 at render time, causing Step 5 Payment to be skipped entirely.
ui_bugs.root_cause: The totalAmount variable (line 115) computed from regFee + accomFee + memFee is 0 when the button condition evaluates, even though the Fee Breakdown section displays ₹4000. This is a React state/memo timing issue where the button text condition runs before useMemo values are recalculated.

integration_issues.issue: Step 5 (PaymentPage) is never rendered - registration goes directly from Review to Success page bypassing payment

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix EventRegistrationPage.jsx - totalAmount is 0 when button text condition evaluates, should show 'Proceed to Payment' when total > 0
Possible fix: Use a local state variable for totalAmount instead of derived useMemo, or add useEffect to track when fees change
Alternative fix: Add a null/loading check before rendering the Review step submit button
critical_code_review_comments: EventRegistrationPage.jsx line 812: Button text condition `totalAmount > 0` evaluates to false when totalAmount should be 4000. The useMemo dependencies may not be triggering re-render in time.
Consider using useState for totalAmount with useEffect to update it when fee values change, ensuring the button text is accurate.
The Fee Breakdown section displays correct values (₹4000) but the button logic uses stale totalAmount value (0)

success_rate.frontend: 80% - Admin Payment Settings working, Event Registration Payment Step BROKEN

seed_data_creation: Multiple test registrations created with names like 'Payment Test XXXXX' - total_amount: 4000, payment_status: pending
context_for_next_testing_agent: Payment system backend APIs all working (UPI QR, UTR submission, admin verify, payment settings). Admin Payment Settings page works correctly - shows bank accounts, UPI IDs, Razorpay status. CRITICAL: Event registration payment flow is broken - Step 5 (PaymentPage) is never rendered because totalAmount evaluates to 0 in the button condition even when fees are displayed as ₹4000. The issue is in EventRegistrationPage.jsx where the computed totalAmount (regFee + accomFee + memFee) is 0 at render time.

backend_apis.GET /api/payments/upi-qr: PASSED - Generates QR code with UPI URL
backend_apis.POST /api/payments/submit-utr: PASSED - Creates payment with status verification_pending
backend_apis.GET /api/admin/payment-settings: PASSED - Returns full settings with razorpay_configured status
backend_apis.PUT /api/admin/payment-settings: PASSED - Updates bank accounts and UPI IDs
backend_apis.POST /api/payments/create-order: PASSED - Creates payment (empty razorpay_order_id since not configured)

frontend_admin.Razorpay status display: PASSED - Shows 'Not Configured' with red indicator
frontend_admin.Bank accounts section: PASSED - Shows SBI account with all details

frontend_registration_payment.Step 0 Fee Overview: PASSED - Shows fee tiers and accommodation fees
frontend_registration_payment.Step 1-3 Registration: PASSED - Participant, Details, Accommodation steps work
frontend_registration_payment.Step 4 Review display: PASSED - Shows correct Fee Breakdown with ₹4000 total
frontend_registration_payment.Step 4 Review button text: FAILED - Shows 'Confirm Registration' instead of 'Proceed to Payment'
frontend_registration_payment.Step 5 Payment page: NOT TESTED - Never rendered due to bug
frontend_registration_payment.Success page: PASSED - Shows correct info with payment status 'Pending (Offline)'

rca_of_issue.bug_description: Event registration Step 5 (Payment) is skipped - goes directly to success page
rca_of_issue.root_cause: In EventRegistrationPage.jsx, the totalAmount variable (line 115: `const totalAmount = regFee + accomFee + memFee`) is computed as 0 when the handleSubmit function runs the condition `if (totalAmount > 0) { setStep(5); }`. This causes the else branch to execute, setting `submitted = true` and bypassing the payment step.
reproduction_steps: 4. Fill participant details and click Next
6. On Review step, observe Fee Breakdown shows Total: ₹4000
7. Observe button text says 'Confirm Registration' (should say 'Proceed to Payment')
8. Click button - goes directly to success page instead of Step 5 Payment
mitigation_suggestions: Option 1: Convert totalAmount from computed value to state variable with useEffect to update when fees change
Option 2: Add explicit fee recalculation in handleSubmit before the condition check
Option 3: Pass the computed fees directly to handleSubmit and use them for the condition instead of totalAmount
